questions.question: Explain why Naive Bayes is called 'naive' and discuss the implications of this assumption.
expectedPoints: The naive assumption refers to conditional independence of features given class
This assumption is rarely true in real text data where words are correlated
Despite this, Naive Bayes often works well in practice for text classification
The assumption simplifies computation and makes the model efficient

questions.question: Describe the step-by-step process of training a Naive Bayes classifier for text classification, including data preparation, probability calculation, and prediction.
expectedPoints: Data preparation: tokenization, lowercasing, stopword removal, stemming
Feature extraction: create vocabulary and document vectors
Calculate prior probabilities: P(C) for each class
Calculate likelihoods: P(w|C) for each word given each class
Apply Laplace smoothing to handle unseen words
Make predictions using log probabilities to avoid underflow
